item.title: Version 1.5.1
item.description:
What's new in 1.5.1

  • Codex usage in Settings — the Usage screen now shows your OpenAI Codex limits right under your Claude ones (5-hour and weekly windows, with reset times). Reads straight from Codex — no API key needed.
  • Switch models mid-chat and keep going — jump from Claude to GPT-5.6 (or back) partway through a conversation and the new model is caught up automatically. It gets a quick summary of what you've been working on — not the whole transcript — so it picks up right where you left off.

item.title: Version 1.5.0
item.description:
What's new in 1.5.0

  • OpenAI GPT-5.6 is here — Berries Code now runs OpenAI's Codex models right alongside Claude. Pick GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, or Luna from the model menu. Uses your ChatGPT sign-in — no API key needed.
  • Redesigned model picker — the menu now groups Claude and OpenAI Codex clearly, with a provider badge on the chip so you always know which you're on. It closes when you click away, like it should.
  • Switch per chat — each conversation remembers its model, and you can swap between Claude and Codex whenever you like.

item.title: Version 1.4.3
item.description:
What's new in 1.4.3

  • @ menu now shows its results — the @-mention popup was collapsing to an empty box (a zero-height layout bug hid the whole list). It now renders your files, folders, conversations — and Fable Planning Mode.
  • @fable — Fable Planning Mode — type @ and pick Fable Planning Mode. It makes the model plan and reason the Fable way (front-load the spec, plan before acting, verify its own work, lead with the outcome). Built into the app, works in every project.

item.title: Version 1.4.2
item.description:
What's new in 1.4.2

  • @fable — Fable Planning Mode — type @ and pick Fable Planning Mode at the top of the menu. It makes the model plan and reason the Fable way (front-load the spec, plan before acting, verify its own work, lead with the outcome) on whatever model you're running. Built into the app — no file needed, works in every project.

item.title: Version 1.4.1
item.description:
What's new in 1.4.1

  • File panel — no more top gap — the dead space above the file tree is gone. The project name now sits flush at the top.
  • Cmd+F to search files — press Cmd+F (or click the magnifying glass) in the file panel to instantly search across all files with fuzzy matching. Results show the file name and its folder path. Click to open, right-click to attach to chat.

item.title: Version 1.4.0
item.description:
What's new in 1.4.0

  • File viewer & editor — click the new panel icon in the toolbar to open your project's file tree. Click any file to read it. Edit and save with Cmd+S. Works for .env files, code, configs — anything text. Images show a preview. Right-click any file to attach it to the chat.
  • Attach large files and folders — attached files no longer get pasted as raw text. The path is sent instead and Claude reads the file directly, so size limits are gone and you can attach folders too.
  • Smarter file search — @ mentions and the + menu now use fuzzy matching (type "cvm" to find "ChatViewModel.swift"). Results are ranked by relevance, not just alphabetically.

item.title: Version 1.3.12
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.12

  • Attach any file type — drag a .pdf, .swift, .zip, .env, or anything else onto the chat and it becomes an attachment chip. Previously only images worked.
  • Paste screenshots with Cmd+V — take a screenshot to your clipboard (Cmd+Ctrl+Shift+4) then Cmd+V straight into the chat. No drag-and-drop required.
  • .env and hidden files are now visible — the @ mention search and the + file menu now show dotfiles like .env and .gitignore. node_modules and .git are still hidden.

item.title: Version 1.3.10
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.10

  • Chat text follows your theme now — switching between light and dark themes used to leave old messages stuck in their previous color (dark-on-dark or white-on-light). Messages now recolor instantly when you change themes.
  • Easier on the eyes in dark mode — regular message text is slightly softened on dark themes so it doesn't glare; bold text and headings stay bright so they stand out.

item.title: Version 1.3.8
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.8

  • The text you type scales too — the "Text Size" setting used to only enlarge the messages you read, leaving the input box tiny. Now the box where you type grows with the same setting, so you can actually read what you're writing.

item.title: Version 1.3.7
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.7

  • Conversations actually work now — projectless chats used to reply with nothing (they were locked to "ask first" mode with no way to approve anything, so every action was denied and the chat looked frozen). They now start with Full Access so Claude can read, edit, and run commands right away.
  • Access control in the titlebar — a new shield menu (top-right, shown only in a Conversation) lets you switch between Safe (read-only, locked down), Full Access, and Turbo whenever you want.

item.title: Version 1.3.6
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.6

  • Unsent text is no longer lost — if you type a message and switch to another chat before sending, your draft is now kept and restored when you come back. It even survives quitting and reopening the app.

item.title: Version 1.3.5
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.5

  • Conversations section placed like Antigravity — it now flows directly below your projects list (not stuck at the very bottom), and the header has a + button to start a new conversation right from the section.

item.title: Version 1.3.4
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.4

  • Conversations list moved to the bottom of the sidebar — projectless chats now live in their own pinned section beneath your projects (like it should have been in 1.3.3), and the header and empty state are properly left-aligned.

item.title: Version 1.3.3
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.3

  • Start a conversation without a project — a new New Conversation button at the top of the sidebar lets you chat with Claude straight away, no folder required. These projectless chats collect in their own Conversations list lower in the sidebar, separate from your projects.

item.title: Version 1.3.2
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.2

  • Adjustable chat text size — a new Text Size control in Settings → App lets you make the messages and replies in the chat area larger (up to 160%), so they're easier to read on a smaller or further-away screen. The sidebar stays the same, and there's a live preview and a one-click reset.

item.title: Version 1.3.1
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.1

  • Fix: chats no longer glitch through the folder name — when you open or close a folder in the sidebar, its chats used to slide up over the folder title for a moment. They now fold neatly away behind the folder header.

item.title: Version 1.3.0
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.0

  • Open multiple project folders at once — opening a folder in the sidebar no longer closes the others. Each open folder shows its own chats, and closing a folder just tidies the drawer without switching your active project.
  • Tidy chat lists — each folder shows its 5 most recent chats. If there are more, a subtle "See all (N)" row expands the full list in place, with "Show less" to tuck it back away. A chat that's still running always stays visible.
  • The drawer remembers itself — which folders you had open is restored when you relaunch the app.

item.title: Version 1.2.17
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.17

  • Subagents you can actually read — click any agent in the drawer to open a full view with its task, complete live activity, and final result. Text is selectable, and it works side by side with the chat.
  • Subagent results land in the chat — when an agent finishes, its result appears as a clearly marked "Subagent result" card, and the lead agent gets it with your next message.
  • No more lost results — the final answer of every subagent is now captured instead of being thrown away, and activity lines are no longer cut off.

item.title: Version 1.2.16
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.16

  • Each chat remembers its own model — picking a model in one chat no longer changes it in your other chats. Every conversation keeps its own choice (also across app restarts); new chats simply start on the model you used last.

item.title: Version 1.2.15
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.15

  • Fix: the working dot no longer bounces — the little pulsing dot next to "Conjuring…", "Working…" etc. could start bouncing up and down like a ball, especially with two projects working at the same time. Its pulse animation was accidentally grabbing onto layout movement; it's now locked to fading only. Same fix for the yellow dot in the sidebar.

item.title: Version 1.2.14
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.14

  • Fix: no more bouncing chat — the little dot next to the thinking label sat in a chat area that wobbled up and down while working (introduced in 1.2.13 by the new sidebar pulse animation). The chat is steady again; the sidebar dot still pulses.

item.title: Version 1.2.13
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.13

  • Chats keep working when you switch away — a running task no longer looks abandoned when you hop to another chat. Come back and it's still live: streaming text, the working animation, and the red stop button, right where you left them.
  • Pulsing dot in the sidebar — any conversation still working in the background shows a small pulsing yellow dot, so you always know something is cooking.
  • Done notifications always arrive — you now get the finished notification even if you switched to another chat mid-task.
  • Deleting a conversation now also stops any task it was still running.

item.title: Version 1.2.12
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.12

  • Fix: scroll freeze permanently gone — replaced the markdown parser with a custom fast renderer that runs synchronously (no background threads, no re-renders). Switched from LazyVStack to VStack so scroll heights are always exact.
  • Fix: old chats now reliably open at the bottom — waits for layout to settle before scrolling, so you always land on the newest message.

item.title: Version 1.2.11
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.11

  • Fix: scroll freeze actually gone — rows now render at their final height the moment they appear. No more layout jumping, no more main thread stalls during scroll.

item.title: Version 1.2.10
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.10

  • Fix: old chats now open at the bottom — opening a previous chat now correctly lands you at the most recent message.

item.title: Version 1.2.9
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.9

  • Fix: scroll freeze properly solved — all text rendering (markdown parsing, syntax highlighting) now runs on a background thread. The main thread does nothing while rows load during scroll.

item.title: Version 1.2.8
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.8

  • Fix: scroll freeze completely gone — text rendering and syntax highlighting now happen off the main thread as rows load while scrolling. The app stays smooth no matter how long the chat is.

item.title: Version 1.2.7
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.7

  • Fix: long chats no longer freeze — opening an old chat with hundreds of messages is now instant. Only the last 150 messages load on open; a "Show earlier messages" button reveals the rest on demand.
  • Faster scrolling — the chat feed does far less work while you scroll through a long conversation.
  • Typing no longer slows down big chats — keystrokes in the input box no longer re-render the entire message feed.

item.title: Version 1.2.6
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.6

  • Strawberry menu bar icon — the menu bar icon is now the Berries Code strawberry silhouette, and correctly switches between black and white based on your menu bar style.

item.title: Version 1.2.5
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.5

  • Fix: Plugins row alignment — the Plugins sidebar row now lines up exactly with Settings.

item.title: Version 1.2.4
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.4

  • Plugin Store — browse, search, and install Claude Code plugins without touching the terminal. Find it in the sidebar under your projects (puzzle-piece icon). Filter by category, search by name or keyword, and install with one click. Installed plugins show a checkmark and can be uninstalled from the card or the detail view.

item.title: Version 1.2.3
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.3

  • Select text mode — a new "Select text" button at the top-right of the chat turns the conversation into one continuous document, so you can drag-select any part (or all) of a chat and copy it. Copied text comes out labeled You:/Claude: so it stays readable wherever you paste it.
  • Clean chats — internal machinery (skill instructions, plugin/hook notes) no longer shows up inside your conversations.

item.title: Version 1.2.2
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.2

  • Fix: chats disappeared from the sidebar after 1.2.1 — the background-session filter was too broad and also hid chats created by this app. No data was lost; all chats reappear after this update.

item.title: Version 1.2.1
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.1

  • No more phantom chats — background security-review sessions created by Claude Code plugins no longer appear in the sidebar.
  • Freeze fix — the sidebar no longer re-reads entire session files on every refresh; large sessions can't lock up the app anymore.

item.title: Version 1.2.0
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.0

  • Drag & drop images — drop an image anywhere in the app and it attaches as a chip, exactly like the + button. Works with Finder files, screenshot thumbnails, and images dragged from browsers.
  • Dropping an image on the message box no longer pastes the file path as text.
  • A "Drop image to attach" highlight shows while you're dragging.
